items.title: Maternal inheritance suppresses CAA and amyloid plaques in 5xFAD (Tg7031)/APOE4 mice
items.author: Fareeha Saadi, Ilia V. Katritch, Samira Parhizkar, Brisa Lugo, Yoonho Cho, Jason D. Ulrich, David M. Holtzman
items.content: <article>In their recent study, Sasmita et al.1 reported that the parental origin of the 5xFAD (Tg6799) transgene substantially influences amyloid-β (Aβ) plaque deposition in this widely used amyloid model for this aspect of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) pathology. Mice inheriting the transgene from the male parent exhibited approximately twice the plaque burden compared to those with maternal inheritance. This effect was consistent across multiple breeding colonies and independent of maternal care or in utero environment, indicating a robust and reproducible influence of parental origin on pathology.</article>

items.title: Negative results positive for understanding the neural representation of valence across the brain
items.author: Sounak Mohanta, Kay M. Tye
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Biane et al. used cellular-resolution recordings in ventral CA1 (vCA1) to comprehensively establish that it encodes stimulus features and identity, but not a generalized abstraction of valence. This suggests a complementary role of vCA1 to valence coding regions by providing stimulus-specific representations.</article>

items.title: Sensory learning induces circuit-level redistribution of AMPARs in the barrel cortex
items.author: Xiaonan Li, Simon X. Chen
items.content: <article>Recent advances in genetic techniques have allowed visualization of AMPAR dynamics and identification of memory-related neurons in the brain. Li et al. report in Neuron that sensory learning induces circuit-level redistribution of GluA1 within memory-related neuronal ensembles in the barrel cortex.</article>

items.title: Branch logic: Dendritic computations diversify inhibition
items.author: Sabine Rannio, Shawniya Alageswaran, P. Jesper Sjöström
items.content: <article>Long known for different circuit roles, parvalbumin and somatostatin interneurons are shown by Morabito et al., in this issue of Neuron, to differ in dendritic integration strategies. This cell-type-specific branch logic—sublinear versus supralinear—arises from synapse location and composition, shaping inhibitory timing in the cortex.</article>

items.title: Unlocking the epigenome: Single-cell histone profiling in cortical development
items.author: Yong-Qi Gao, Fides Zenk
items.content: <article>Ditzer et al. used single-cell cytometry by time-of-flight-based method for epigenetic analysis to profile histone modifications in the primary human cortex and cortical organoids, revealing how chromatin states, particularly H3K27me3, govern neural progenitor fate. This study highlights the potential of multiplexed epigenomics in understanding human brain development.</article>

items.title: The “five-star matrix” for patient-centric drug discovery and development in neuroscience
items.author: Axel Pähler, Eoin C. O’Connor, Hayley Binch, Sherri Dudal, Luca C. Gobbi, Thomas Lübbers, Andrés Olivares-Morales, Rosa María Rodríguez Sarmiento, Uwe Grether, Martin Stahl, Gennaro Pagano, Antonia F. Stepan
items.content: <article>Stepan et al. introduce the “five-star matrix,” a comprehensive framework linking biomarkers across dimensions and systems. This patient-centric approach fosters collaboration and supports end-to-end translational strategies to improve success in neuroscience drug development.</article>
